chunk_summary: This section addresses barrier-focused management of constipation, GERD, asthma, and skin-fold diseases in the context of obesity-related comorbidities, emphasizing screening, lifestyle integration, and evidence-based interventions.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of screening for non-metabolic obesity complications like constipation, GERD, asthma, and skin-fold diseases.
Learn evidence-based management strategies for constipation, GERD, asthma, and hidradenitis suppurativa.
Identify red flags and indications for specialist referral in obesity-related comorbidities.
Incorporate lifestyle modifications into the management of obesity-related conditions.
key_facts: Constipation management includes adequate fluids, fiber, toilet routines, and evidence-based laxatives; red flags include blood in stool, weight loss, and severe abdominal distension.
GERD management emphasizes earlier dinner timing, avoiding late-night snacking, and addressing weight-neutral triggers; alarm symptoms like dysphagia or hematemesis require specialist evaluation.
Asthma control involves guideline-based controller therapy, proper inhaler technique, and a graduated activity plan to improve exercise tolerance.
Skin-fold disease management includes reducing friction and moisture to prevent intertrigo and hidradenitis suppurativa.
Obesity-related comorbidities like GERD and asthma can disrupt sleep and physical activity, contributing to perceived non-adherence.
Evidence-based supportive care measures are essential
reference_quote: Management of functional constipation in children includes adequate fluids, fiber, and evidence-based laxative therapy (Reference 1).
